display.purpose: The hero wordmark only — Inter 800, fluid to 68px.

body.purpose: Section ledes and prose, capped ~64ch, line-height 1.65.

mono.purpose: Inline code, model ids, sizes, chips, the architecture flow.

shadows.name: hero-lift
shadows.purpose: The phone mockup — deep drop plus an iris glow so it reads as lit by the spectral environment.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Iris-tinted bloom that appears only on demo-card hover.

shadows.name: lightbox
shadows.purpose: Full-resolution screenshot viewer floating over a blurred backdrop.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover, lift, and reveal — no bounce.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Color/border state changes (links, footer, close button).

motion.name: live-pulse
motion.purpose: The amber status dot's expanding-halo pulse; collapses under prefers-reduced-motion.

breakpoints.name: stack
breakpoints.purpose: Hero grid, gallery, and 2-col blocks collapse to a single column; the phone mockup moves above the copy.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The lead call to action — iris fill, near-black ink. Used for 'View source'.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; padding: 12px 20px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid transparent; background: var(--iris-bright, oklch(0.79 0.15 288)); color: var(--ink-on-accent, oklch(0.17 0.03 286)); text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), background 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); background: var(--iris, oklch(0.72 0.16 286)); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — surface film with a hairline edge that warms to iris on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; padding: 12px 20px; border-radius: 999px; border: 1px solid var(--border-strong, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.2)); background: var(--surface, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.045)); color: var(--text, oklch(0.97 0.004 280)); text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 0.18s ease, background 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); background: var(--surface-2, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.08)); border-color: var(--iris-border, oklch(0.72 0.16 286 / 0.45)); }

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Signature brand pill with a pulsing amber live-dot — the one warm signal in the system.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text-2, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.72)); background: var(--surface, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.045)); border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.1)); border-radius: 999px; padding: 6px 14px; } .ds-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--amber, oklch(0.82 0.13 73)); box-shadow: 0 0 10px 1px var(--amber, oklch(0.82 0.13 73)); animation: ds-live-pulse 2.6s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1) infinite; } @keyframes ds-live-pulse { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0.55); } 70% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 7px oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0); } 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-dot { animation: none; box-shadow: 0 0 10px 1px var(--amber, oklch(0.82 0.13 73)); } }

components.name: Demo Card
components.description: Gallery card: flat at rest, iris-tinted bloom and lift on hover, amber DEMO badge, iris bullet markers.
components.html:
DEMO 01

Basic GLB Viewer

One model, default image-based lighting, a gentle auto-spin.

  • FilamentScene / FilamentView setup
  • Loading a .glb with <Model>

BoomBox · CC0
components.css: .ds-card { width: 320px; background: var(--elevated, oklch(0.215 0.024 283)); border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.1)); border-radius: 18px; overflow: hidden; display: flex; flex-direction: column; transition: border-color 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: var(--iris-border, oklch(0.72 0.16 286 / 0.45)); transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 24px 60px -34px oklch(0.68 0.19 290 / 0.5); } .ds-card-shot { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 16 / 10; background: linear-gradient(160deg, var(--elevated, oklch(0.215 0.024 283)), var(--base, oklch(0.15 0.02 280))); } .ds-badge { position: absolute; top: 12px; left: 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--amber, oklch(0.82 0.13 73)); background: oklch(0.15 0.02 280 / 0.72); border: 1px solid var(--amber-border, oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0.4)); border-radius: 8px; padding: 4px 9px; backdrop-filter: blur(6px); } .ds-card-body { padding: 20px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; } .ds-card-body h3 { margin: 0; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text, oklch(0.97 0.004 280)); } .ds-tagline { margin: 0; font-size: 14.5px; color: var(--text-2, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.72)); } .ds-demos { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-demos li { position: relative; padding-left: 20px; font-size: 13.5px; color: var(--text-2, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.72)); } .ds-demos li::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 2px; top: 8px; width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 2px; background: var(--iris, oklch(0.72 0.16 286)); } .ds-model { margin: 0; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-3, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.56)); border-top: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.1)); padding-top: 12px; }

components.name: License Chip (CC0)
components.description: Semantic license tag; the CC0 / permissive variant switches to Permissive Green.
components.css: .ds-lic { display: inline-block; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11.5px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--surface-2, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.08)); border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(1 0 0 / 0.1)); color: var(--text-2, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.72)); } .ds-lic-cc0 { color: var(--license, oklch(0.83 0.14 158)); border-color: oklch(0.83 0.14 158 / 0.32); }

components.name: Attribution Note
components.description: Callout with a full hairline border and amber tint — deliberately NOT a side-stripe.
components.html: Fox attribution: model by PixelMannen (CC0 1.0); rigging & animation by tomkranis (CC BY 4.0).
components.css: .ds-note { padding: 16px 18px; border: 1px solid var(--amber-border, oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0.4)); background: var(--amber-soft, oklch(0.82 0.13 73 / 0.14)); border-radius: 12px; color: var(--text-2, oklch(0.97 0.004 280 / 0.72)); font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.65; }

narrative.overview: A darkened studio bay where each 3D model is mounted, lit, and presented. The interface is the matte, instrument-grade equipment that surrounds a lit subject — and the page itself is lit the way the demos are: by a spectral environment, the web echo of the image-based lighting (IBL) that lights every render inside the app. Precision and cinema in one surface: a faint blueprint grid of crosshair ticks carries the engineering exactness, while a prism-spectrum bloom carries the light. The system commits to one cool near-black world and lets a full spectrum live in the atmosphere rather than the chrome; the hero is washed by a bloom that sweeps teal to iris to magenta to amber, anchored to the top of each page and dissolving as the reader descends. It is not a clone of the upstream react-native-filament banner that inspired it — it borrows the spectral-render energy and makes it this showcase's own. The demos are always the hero; nothing decorative competes with a render.
keyCharacteristics: Cool near-black surface; the color story lives in the atmosphere, not the UI chrome.
A spectral bloom (teal-iris-magenta-amber) anchored to the hero, fading downward.
A faint blueprint grid of crosshair ticks plus film grain for instrument-grade texture.
One iris primary; amber rationed as the single warm signal.
Flat surfaces, hairline borders, tonal layering — depth without heavy shadow.

rules.name: The Single Filament Rule
rules.body: Iris is the only interactive color and amber is the only warm signal; both are rationed. If a surface needs a third 'look at me' color, the answer is hierarchy, not hue.

rules.name: The Spectrum-Stays-In-The-Air Rule
rules.body: The full prism lives in the atmosphere layer (bloom) and nowhere else. A spectral color on a button, a border, or body text is forbidden — it belongs to the light, not the object.

rules.name: The Earned-Mono Rule
rules.body: Monospace appears only where the content is literally code, an identifier, a version, or a license. It is never a costume for 'this feels technical.'

rules.name: The No-Gradient-Text Rule
rules.body: Display emphasis comes from weight, size, and a single solid color (iris) — never from background-clip: text over a gradient.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat with a hairline border at rest. Shadow is a response to state (hover, float, lit) — if a static card has a drop shadow, it's wrong.

dos: Do keep the spectrum in the air. Spend the prism on the atmosphere bloom; keep working UI to iris + amber + neutrals.
Do ration amber to a single warm signal (live dot, DEMO 0x badge). Scarcity is the point.
Do build depth from tonal layers (base, base-2, elevated) and hairline borders; reserve shadow for genuinely-lifted/lit elements.
Do keep every text color at WCAG AA on its surface — text-3 is the floor at 56% opacity; verify any new muted tone hits at least 4.5:1.
Do present each render full-bleed in its tile, the model centered and unobstructed; the demo is always the hero.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion path — the auto-spin video pauses and exposes controls; the live-dot pulse and hover transitions collapse.
Do mark new sections with the spectral hairline divider, not a text eyebrow.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text over a gradient). Emphasis is weight, size, and a single solid iris.
Don't use a border-left/border-right color stripe greater than 1px on cards, notes, or callouts. Use a full hairline border plus a tint.
Don't stack a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section — lead with the headline and let the spectral divider signal the section.
Don't let it read as a generic SaaS landing page: no gradient-blob hero, no identical icon+heading+text card grid, no pricing tiers, no 'trusted by' logo wall.
Don't let it read as a plain GitHub README: technical content must be composed, with hierarchy and the renders doing visual work.
Don't drift into crypto/AI-hype (decorative glassmorphism, gradients on everything) or toy/gamey styling — either undercuts the engineering credibility.
Don't imply the renders run in the browser. The site documents a native-GPU (Metal) runtime; keep the phone/native framing honest.
Don't put a spectral hue on a button, border, or body text — color belongs to the light, not the object.
